papers.title_en: GAP-GDRNet: Geometry-Aware Monocular Visual Pose Sensing on a Single-Target Synthetic Spacecraft Dataset
papers.evidence: 航天器6D位姿估计可支撑卫星组件精密对准
papers.tldr: 航天器图像常因弱纹理、薄附件、遮挡与光照变化导致稀疏几何证据，单目位姿感知面临挑战。本文提出GAP-GDRNet，在GDR-Net框架中引入注意力特征精炼AFR与补丁几何自注意力PGSA模块，增强几何结构感知。在Blender合成的单目标航天器数据集上验证了位姿估计有效性。该方法为非合作交会对接提供了更鲁棒的视觉感知方案。
papers.abstract_en: Monocular relative pose sensing is a central perception problem in non-cooperative rendezvous and on-orbit servicing. In spacecraft images, however, weak surface texture, thin appendages, illumination changes, and partial occlusion often leave only sparse and unstable geometric evidence. This article presents GAP-GDRNet, a geometry-aware attention-enhanced framework for monocular RGB-based 6D pose sensing. The method follows the geometry-guided direct regression paradigm of GDR-Net and modifies two points in the pipeline: an attention-based feature refinement (AFR) module is placed before dense geometric prediction, and a patch-level geometric self-attention (PGSA) module is inserted into Patch-PnP. AFR reinforces global spacecraft structure together with local weak-texture cues; PGSA then relates downsampled geometric patches before final pose regression. A Blender-based annotation process supplies target masks, visible-region masks, dense model-coordinate maps, camera intrinsics, and 6D pose labels for supervised training.
